text_blocks.text: Holy Cross Father John Francis O'Hara was
elected vice-president in 1933 and president of Notre
Dame in 1934. During his tenure at Notre Dame, he
brought numerous refugee intellectuals to campus; he
selected Frank H. Spearman, Jeremiah D. M. Ford, Irvin
Abell, and Josephine Brownson for the Laetare Medal,
instituted in 1883. O'Hara strongly believed that the
Fighting Irish football team could be an effective means
to "acquaint the public with the ideals that dominate"
Notre Dame. He wrote, "Notre Dame football is a
spiritual service because it is played for the honor and
glory of God and of his Blessed Mother. When St. Paul
said: Whether you eat or drink, or whatsoever else
you do, do all for the glory of God,' he included
